textDisplay:
Dear Warner Bros.,
I am writing this letter to express my deep dissatisfaction and frustration with the continuous delays and unreliability surrounding the release dates of upcoming movies, particularly the sequel to "Dune." As an avid moviegoer and a passionate fan of the "Dune" franchise, I have patiently awaited the release of "Dune 2" for quite some time. However, the repeated postponements and constant changes in release dates have left me and many others feeling deceived and disheartened.

It is disconcerting to witness a pattern where Warner Bros. announces a release date, only to later change it without clear and reasonable justifications. This lack of transparency and accountability reflects poorly on the integrity of your company and raises doubts about your commitment to delivering quality content to your audience.

Moreover, the disappointing quality of recent films released by Warner Bros. has only added to the frustration. It appears that the company is prioritizing rushed and subpar productions over meeting its promises to the viewers. This not only tarnishes the reputation of beloved franchises but also undermines the trust and loyalty of dedicated fans.

Additionally, it has come to my attention that there are concerns regarding the excessive pricing and restrictive distribution practices of Warner Bros. Your decision to overcharge customers for movie rentals and limit their options by forcing them to purchase films outright demonstrates a disregard for consumer satisfaction and fairness. This exploitative approach reinforces the perception that greed has taken precedence over providing an enjoyable movie experience.

In light of these concerns, I hereby announce my decision to initiate a boycott against Warner Bros. and its affiliated productions. Until Warner Bros. demonstrates a genuine commitment to delivering on promised release dates, improving the quality of its films, and adopting fair pricing and distribution practices, I will refrain from renting or purchasing any movies released by your company.

I urge fellow moviegoers and fans who share my frustration to join me in this boycott. Together, we can send a clear message that unreliable release dates, disappointing movies, and unfair practices will not be tolerated. We, the audience, deserve to be treated with respect and integrity by the entertainment industry.

I sincerely hope that Warner Bros. will take this boycott seriously and address the concerns raised by its loyal supporters. By doing so, you have the opportunity to restore the faith and trust that has been eroded by recent practices.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I trust that you will carefully consider the grievances expressed in this letter and take appropriate actions to rectify the situation. I eagerly anticipate a positive response from Warner Bros., one that reassures fans that their voices are heard and valued.

Yours sincerely,

Casey McClanahan

textDisplay:
Having failed to defeat Aquaman the first time, Black Manta, still driven by the need to avenge his father’s death, will stop at nothing to take Aquaman down once and for all. This time Black Manta is more formidable than ever before, wielding the power of the mythic Black Trident, which unleashes an ancient and malevolent force. To defeat him, Aquaman will turn to his imprisoned brother Orm, the former King of Atlantis, to forge an unlikely alliance. Together, they must set aside their differences in order to protect their kingdom and save Aquaman’s family, and the world, from irreversible destruction.

All returning to the roles they originated, Jason Momoa plays Arthur Curry/Aquaman, now balancing his duties as both the King of Atlantis and a new father; Patrick Wilson is Orm, Aquaman’s half-brother and his nemesis, who must now step into a new role as his brother’s reluctant ally; Amber Heard is Mera, Atlantis’ Queen and mother of the heir to the throne; Yahya Abdul-Mateen II is Black Manta, committed more than ever to avenge his father’s death by destroying Aquaman, his family and Atlantis; and Nicole Kidman as Atlanna, a fierce leader and mother with the heart of a warrior. Also reprising their roles are Dolph Lundgren as King Nereus and Randall Park as Dr. Stephen Shin.

Directed by Wan, “Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom” is produced by Peter Safran, Wan and Rob Cowan. The executive producers are Galen Vaisman and Walter Hamada.

The screenplay is by David Leslie Johnson-McGoldrick, from a story by James Wan & David Leslie Johnson-McGoldrick and Jason Momoa & Thomas Pa’a Sibbett, based on characters from DC, Aquaman created by Paul Norris and Mort Weisinger. 

Joining Wan behind-the-camera is his sterling team of “Aquaman” artisans: director of photography Don Burgess (“The Conjuring 2”), production designer Bill Brzeski (“Jumanji: The Next Level”), editor Kirk Morri (“Furious 7”), composer Rupert Gregson-Williams (“Wonder Woman”) and music supervisor is Michelle Silverman (“Malignant”). Visual effects supervisor Nick Davis (“The Clash of the Titans” films, “The Dark Knight”) and costume designer Richard Sale (“Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness,” “Ready Player One”) also join.

Warner Bros. Pictures Presents An Atomic Monster / A Peter Safran Production of A James Wan Film, “Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom,” set to open in theaters internationally beginning 20 December 2023 and in North America on December 20, 2023; it will be distributed worldwide by Warner Bros. Pictures.
